{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"0cc470b6-d451-4ff7-8e60-2531090eea4b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:15:09.575Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_621fe3d165f543638c280e1d","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n    46→- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n    47→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    48→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    49→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    50→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    51→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    52→\n    53→## React + SQLite\n    54→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    55→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    56→\n    57→## Security\n    58→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    59→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    60→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    61→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    62→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    63→\n    64→## Working Directory\n    65→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    66→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    67→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    68→\n    69→## Security\n    70→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    71→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    72→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    73→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    74→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"4d74ea03-740c-47d8-aacd-7449f2ccc940","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:15:13.157Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":75,"startLine":1,"totalLines":75}}}

XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    63→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    71→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    72→- Email notifications require SMTP env (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 201 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n2. Check issue #21 for Telegram token response\n3. Check production health\n4. 16 rounds of code review done (diminishing returns) — consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n5. ON DELETE CASCADE noted but deferred — manual deletion works, no user deletion feature exists\n","numLines":95,"startLine":1,"totalLines":95}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_29b0a8fddcf3402194ebef6d","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"bc1a36cc-9d92-407e-b5dc-621647f5b252","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:15:13.168Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

    1→# Learned Patterns\\n     2→\\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\\n     5→  ```bash\\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\\n     7→  ```\\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\\n    10→\\n    11→## Session Flow\\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\\n    15→\\n    16→## Environment\\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\\n    23→\\n    24→## Build\\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\\n    30→\\n    31→## Browser Testing\\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \\\"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\\\"`\\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\\\"button[type=button].w-full\\\").click()`\\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\\\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\\\"` needed each session\\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\\n    41→\\n    42→## Deployment\\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\\n    46→- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\\n    47→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\\n    48→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\\n    49→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\\n    50→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\\n    51→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\\n    52→\\n    53→## React + SQLite\\n    54→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \\\"0\\\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\\n    55→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\\n    56→\\n    57→## Security\\n    58→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\\n    59→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\\n    60→- Notification failures should never mask succ","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false,"persistedOutputPath":"/home/claude-beat-2026-03-19-19-40-05/.claude/projects/-root-Projects-bookmate/a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49/tool-results/bnoi2cqad.txt","persistedOutputSize":90129}}

session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49\",\"uuid\":\"8f046881-d129-4859-bf2a-1f20edd2e517\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-24T16:15:09.575Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49\",\"uuid\":\"0cc470b6-d451-4ff7-8e60-2531090eea4b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-24T16:15:09.575Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_start\",\"message\":{\"id\":\"msg_202603250015106536a45fe57c48d4\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"model\":\"glm-4.7\",\"content\":[],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"output_tokens\":0}}},\"session_id\":\"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"23597529-1570-4b12-95c7-1b3c3de888fb\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_start\",\"index\":0,\"content_block\":{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"\",\"signature\":\"34c553b0aefc4366a5371a20\"}},\"session_id\":\"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"a3614e47-ee0d-49b3-9b70-17cfedd62a4b\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":0,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"thinking_delta\",\"thinking\":\"Let\"}},\"session_id\":\"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"070e7376-d7c9-4a1a-a7aa-7cebef14d124\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"index\":0,\"delta\":{\"type\":\"thinking_delta\",\"thinking\":\" {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_300692feeaae4e6bb320129e","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"body\":\"## What's needed\\n\\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\\\"test-instance\\\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\\n- Push commits to the repository\\n- Create/merge pull requests\\n- Manage issues and project board\\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\\n\\n## How to fix\\n\\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\\n   ```\\n   GH_TOKEN=\\u003cnew-token\\u003e\\n   ```\\n4. Update the git remote URL:\\n   ```\\n   git remote set-url origin https://\\u003cnew-token\\u003e@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\\n   ```\\n\\n## Priority\\n\\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.\",\"comments\":[{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: PAT expiry is now ~6 days away (March 27). If the token expires, I won't be able to push code, create PRs, or manage issues. Please renew the PAT at your earliest convenience.\\n\\nTo renew: GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Regenerate \\\"test-instance\\\" token → update the GH_TOKEN in `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"⚠️ **4 days remaining** (today is March 23). PAT expires March 27. Please renew ASAP to avoid disruption to code pushes, PR management, and issue tracking.\\n\\nSteps: GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Regenerate 'test-instance' token → update GH_TOKEN in `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"🚨 **3 days remaining** (today is March 24). PAT expires March 27. Once expired, I will be unable to push code, create PRs, or interact with this repository. Please generate a new PAT and update `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL at your earliest convenience.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"⏰ **Reminder (session 2026-03-24 08:00 UTC)**: PAT expires in ~3 days (around March 27). Token is still working but renewal is urgent. Please follow the steps in the issue description to regenerate.\"}],\"title\":\"Action Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\"}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"f83cece3-c879-4fd7-a61f-c3ae40ebec31","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:16:04.033Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"{\"body\":\"## What's needed\\n\\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\\\"test-instance\\\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\\n- Push commits to the repository\\n- Create/merge pull requests\\n- Manage issues and project board\\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\\n\\n## How to fix\\n\\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\\n   ```\\n   GH_TOKEN=\\u003cnew-token\\u003e\\n   ```\\n4. Update the git remote URL:\\n   ```\\n   git remote set-url origin https://\\u003cnew-token\\u003e@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\\n   ```\\n\\n## Priority\\n\\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.\",\"comments\":[{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: PAT expiry is now ~6 days away (March 27). If the token expires, I won't be able to push code, create PRs, or manage issues. Please renew the PAT at your earliest convenience.\\n\\nTo renew: GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Regenerate \\\"test-instance\\\" token → update the GH_TOKEN in `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"⚠️ **4 days remaining** (today is March 23). PAT expires March 27. Please renew ASAP to avoid disruption to code pushes, PR management, and issue tracking.\\n\\nSteps: GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Regenerate 'test-instance' token → update GH_TOKEN in `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"🚨 **3 days remaining** (today is March 24). PAT expires March 27. Once expired, I will be unable to push code, create PRs, or interact with this repository. Please generate a new PAT and update `.claude-beat/.env` and the git remote URL at your earliest convenience.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"⏰ **Reminder (session 2026-03-24 08:00 UTC)**: PAT expires in ~3 days (around March 27). Token is still working but renewal is urgent. Please follow the steps in the issue description to regenerate.\"}],\"title\":\"Action Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\"}","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7d62921ae7f84196aa9e396a","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"body\":\"## What's needed\\nA Telegram Bot Token to enable the automatic Telegram group creation feature (PR #20).\\n\\n## How to get it\\n1. Open Telegram and message [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather)\\n2. Send `/newbot`\\n3. Follow the prompts to name the bot (e.g., \\\"Bookmate Bot\\\" / `bookmate_reading_bot`)\\n4. Copy the API token (format: `123456:ABC-DEF...`)\\n\\n## Where to put it\\nSet these environment variables in the deployment configuration:\\n\\n```\\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=\\u003cyour-bot-token\\u003e\\nTELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL=https://\\u003cyour-domain\\u003e/api/telegram/webhook\\nTELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET=\\u003crandom-string-for-security\\u003e\\nTELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET=\\u003crandom-string-for-setup-endpoint\\u003e\\n```\\n\\n## After setup\\nCall the setup endpoint to register the webhook:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST https://\\u003cyour-domain\\u003e/api/telegram/setup \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"secret\\\": \\\"\\u003cTELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET\\u003e\\\"}'\\n```\\n\\n## Priority\\nNot blocking — the app works fine without it (manual Telegram setup is the fallback). But this enables the full P2 automatic group creation feature.\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\",\"comments\":[{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: This issue still needs a human to provide the Telegram bot token (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN). The auto-Telegram feature (#11) has been merged but cannot function without this credential. Please see the issue description for setup steps.\"},{\"author\":\"XanderZhu\",\"body\":\"@TommyBrennan \\nThe app haven't deployed yet and I can't setup TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL. Deploy the app yourself, choose the platform yourself. Ask me if your need any credentials or account.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: This issue is still waiting for a human to provide the Telegram bot token. The auto-group creation feature cannot function without it. Please provide the TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN when available.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"**Update:** The app is now deployed and live at http://89.167.127.85:3000\\n\\nThe Telegram webhook URL for bot configuration would be:\\n```\\nhttp://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook\\n```\\n\\nWhen you're ready to set up the bot:\\n1. Create a Telegram bot via @BotFather and get the token\\n2. Set these env vars in `app/.env.local`:\\n   - `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=\\u003cyour-token\\u003e`\\n   - `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL=http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook`\\n3. Hit the setup endpoint: `curl -X POST http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/setup`\\n\\nLet me know once you have the bot token and I'll configure it!\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: The app is deployed and live at http://89.167.127.85:3000. The Telegram webhook URL is `http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook`. Whenever you're ready, please create a Telegram bot via @BotFather and share the token so auto-group creation can be enabled.\"}],\"title\":\"Setup: Telegram bot token needed for auto-group creation\"}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"66c166d9-5e7e-425c-9071-2da26825a6de","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:16:05.141Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"{\"body\":\"## What's needed\\nA Telegram Bot Token to enable the automatic Telegram group creation feature (PR #20).\\n\\n## How to get it\\n1. Open Telegram and message [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather)\\n2. Send `/newbot`\\n3. Follow the prompts to name the bot (e.g., \\\"Bookmate Bot\\\" / `bookmate_reading_bot`)\\n4. Copy the API token (format: `123456:ABC-DEF...`)\\n\\n## Where to put it\\nSet these environment variables in the deployment configuration:\\n\\n```\\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=\\u003cyour-bot-token\\u003e\\nTELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL=https://\\u003cyour-domain\\u003e/api/telegram/webhook\\nTELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET=\\u003crandom-string-for-security\\u003e\\nTELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET=\\u003crandom-string-for-setup-endpoint\\u003e\\n```\\n\\n## After setup\\nCall the setup endpoint to register the webhook:\\n```bash\\ncurl -X POST https://\\u003cyour-domain\\u003e/api/telegram/setup \\\\\\n  -H \\\"Content-Type: application/json\\\" \\\\\\n  -d '{\\\"secret\\\": \\\"\\u003cTELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET\\u003e\\\"}'\\n```\\n\\n## Priority\\nNot blocking — the app works fine without it (manual Telegram setup is the fallback). But this enables the full P2 automatic group creation feature.\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\",\"comments\":[{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: This issue still needs a human to provide the Telegram bot token (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN). The auto-Telegram feature (#11) has been merged but cannot function without this credential. Please see the issue description for setup steps.\"},{\"author\":\"XanderZhu\",\"body\":\"@TommyBrennan \\nThe app haven't deployed yet and I can't setup TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL. Deploy the app yourself, choose the platform yourself. Ask me if your need any credentials or account.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: This issue is still waiting for a human to provide the Telegram bot token. The auto-group creation feature cannot function without it. Please provide the TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN when available.\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"**Update:** The app is now deployed and live at http://89.167.127.85:3000\\n\\nThe Telegram webhook URL for bot configuration would be:\\n```\\nhttp://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook\\n```\\n\\nWhen you're ready to set up the bot:\\n1. Create a Telegram bot via @BotFather and get the token\\n2. Set these env vars in `app/.env.local`:\\n   - `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=\\u003cyour-token\\u003e`\\n   - `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL=http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook`\\n3. Hit the setup endpoint: `curl -X POST http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/setup`\\n\\nLet me know once you have the bot token and I'll configure it!\"},{\"author\":\"TommyBrennan\",\"body\":\"Reminder: The app is deployed and live at http://89.167.127.85:3000. The Telegram webhook URL is `http://89.167.127.85:3000/api/telegram/webhook`. Whenever you're ready, please create a Telegram bot via @BotFather and share the token so auto-group creation can be enabled.\"}],\"title\":\"Setup: Telegram bot token needed for auto-group creation\"}","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

The PRD sync is complete - no missing issues.\n\nNow let me write my triage summary for the session and decide what to do:\n\n**Production Status:** Healthy\n- Production site is live at http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint returns healthy status\n- Database connected\n- PM2 process running (uptime ~33 minutes)\n\n**Follow-ups:**\n- Issue #54 (PAT expires ~March 27): ~3 days remaining, still needs human action\n- Issue #21 (Telegram bot token): still waiting for human to provide token\n\n**Work Options:**\n1. All PRD features implemented\n4. 18 rounds of code review completed (diminishing returns)\n\n**Decision:**\nSince there's no approved work to do, I should either:\n1. {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_2026032500162015c3fbf6f9864c69","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## 3. DECIDE - Triage Summary\n\n**Production Status:** ✅ Healthy\n- Production site live at http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: healthy, DB connected\n- PM2 process online (33m uptime)\n\n**Follow-ups:**\n- Issue #54 (PAT expires ~March 27): ⚠️ ~3 days remaining - URGENT\n- Issue #21 (Telegram bot token): Still waiting\n\n**Current State:**\n- All PRD features: ✅ Implemented & deployed\n- All approved issues: ✅ Completed\n- No open PRs\n- 18 rounds of code review completed (diminishing returns)\n\n**What I'll work on:**\nSince no approved work exists and 18 code review rounds show diminishing returns, I'll create a proposal for **E2E testing with Playwright**. This is a valuable infrastructure investment that:\n- Validates critical user flows end-to-end\n- Complements the existing 201 unit tests\n- Uses Playwright (agent-browser is already configured)\n\nLet me create the proposal:"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"be596dad-dde1-49e2-ad7d-cb47d8f29006"}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_start","index":2,"content_block":{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_07336e727d3f4828a4b3c2fe","name":"Bash","input":{}}},"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"f3a1cb7b-062f-4ba7-8a0c-6d3d1b7416d2"}
{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"command\":\"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\\\\n  --title \\\"proposal: Add E2E testing with Playwright\\\" \\\\\\n  --label \\\"proposal\\\" \\\\\\n  --body \\\"## Proposal: End-to-End Testing with Playwright\\n\\n### Why\\nThe project has 201 unit tests providing good coverage of individual API routes and functions. However, there are no end-to-end tests that validate complete user flows through the application.\\n\\nE2E tests would:\\n- Catch integration issues that unit tests miss\\n- Validate critical user journeys work correctly\\n- Provide confidence before deployments\\n- Document expected application behavior\\n- Complement the existing test suite\\n\\n### Proposed Implementation\\nAdd Playwright for E2E testing of critical flows:\\n\\n1. **Authentication Flow**\\n   - User registration\\n   - User login\\n   - User logout\\n   - Protected route redirects\\n\\n2. **Listing Management Flow**\\n   - Create a new listing (with book search)\\n   - Browse listings\\n   - View listing details\\n   - Edit own listing\\n   - Delete own listing\\n\\n3. **Group Formation Flow**\\n   - Join a listing\\n   - Listing closes when full\\n   - Verify notifications sent\\n\\n4. **Profile Flow**\\n   - View profile\\n   - Update profile\\n\\n### Tech Details\\n- **Tool**: Playwright (already available via agent-browser)\\n- **Test config**: \\\\`playwright.config.ts\\\\` in \\\\`app/\\\\` directory\\n- **Test files**: \\\\`app/e2e/\\\\` directory\\n- **Scripts**: Add \\\\`npm run test:e2e\\\\` command\\n- **CI**: Run E2E tests in GitHub Actions\\n\\n### Success Criteria\\n- All critical user flows have E2E coverage\\n- Tests run in CI (with fallback on failure)\\n- Documentation on running tests locally\\n\\n### Priority\\n**P1** — Important infrastructure investment, improves deployment confidence and catches integration bugs early.\\n\\n---\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\\\"\",\"description\":\"Create E2E testing proposal\"}"}},"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"7fb702e8-a70d-433f-b60a-27cebcb4332e"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_2026032500162015c3fbf6f9864c69","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_07336e727d3f4828a4b3c2fe","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\n  --title \"proposal: Add E2E testing with Playwright\" \\\n  --label \"proposal\" \\\n  --body \"## Proposal: End-to-End Testing with Playwright\n\n### Why\nThe project has 201 unit tests providing good coverage of individual API routes and functions. However, there are no end-to-end tests that validate complete user flows through the application.\n\nE2E tests would:\n- Catch integration issues that unit tests miss\n- Validate critical user journeys work correctly\n- Provide confidence before deployments\n- Document expected application behavior\n- Complement the existing test suite\n\n### Proposed Implementation\nAdd Playwright for E2E testing of critical flows:\n\n1. **Authentication Flow**\n   - User registration\n   - User login\n   - User logout\n   - Protected route redirects\n\n2. **Listing Management Flow**\n   - Create a new listing (with book search)\n   - Browse listings\n   - View listing details\n   - Edit own listing\n   - Delete own listing\n\n3. **Group Formation Flow**\n   - Join a listing\n   - Listing closes when full\n   - Verify notifications sent\n\n4. **Profile Flow**\n   - View profile\n   - Update profile\n\n### Tech Details\n- **Tool**: Playwright (already available via agent-browser)\n- **Test config**: \\`playwright.config.ts\\` in \\`app/\\` directory\n- **Test files**: \\`app/e2e/\\` directory\n- **Scripts**: Add \\`npm run test:e2e\\` command\n- **CI**: Run E2E tests in GitHub Actions\n\n### Success Criteria\n- All critical user flows have E2E coverage\n- Tests run in CI (with fallback on failure)\n- Documentation on running tests locally\n\n### Priority\n**P1** — Important infrastructure investment, improves deployment confidence and catches integration bugs early.\n\n---\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\"","description":"Create E2E testing proposal"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"f0c760c2-a407-4951-9845-0f58ecbcf9fe"}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"command\":\"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\\\\n  --title \\\"proposal: Performance optimization and monitoring\\\" \\\\\\n  --label \\\"proposal\\\" \\\\\\n  --body \\\"## Proposal: Performance Optimization and Monitoring\\n\\n### Why\\nAs the application grows and gains users, performance becomes increasingly important. Currently, there's no visibility into:\\n- API response times\\n- Database query performance\\n- N+1 query issues\\n- Frontend rendering performance\\n- Resource usage patterns\\n\\n### Proposed Implementation\\n\\n#### Phase 1: Backend Performance\\n1. **Add response time logging** to API routes\\n2. **Database query logging** (slow query detection >100ms)\\n3. **Add connection pooling** for SQLite (better-sqlite3-pool)\\n4. **Optimize common queries** (add indexes where needed)\\n\\n#### Phase 2: Frontend Performance\\n1. **Add Next.js build-time analysis** (\\\\`@next/bundle-analyzer\\\\`)\\n2. **Optimize images** (use Next.js Image component properly)\\n3. **Code splitting** for heavy components\\n4. **Add loading skeletons** for better perceived performance\\n\\n#### Phase 3: Monitoring\\n1. **Add performance metrics endpoint** (\\\\`/api/performance\\\\`)\\n2. **Track key metrics**: \\n   - Average response time by route\\n   - Database query stats\\n   - Memory usage\\n   - Request rate\\n3. **Add alerting** for performance degradation\\n\\n### Success Criteria\\n- API response times < 200ms (p95)\\n- Database queries < 50ms (p95)\\n- Bundle size reduced by 20%+ (if possible)\\n- Performance metrics visible in dashboard\\n\\n### Priority\\n**P2** — Nice to have. The app performs well now, but this prepares it for scale and provides visibility into performance regressions.\\n\\n---\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\\\"\",\"description\":\"Create performance optimization proposal\"}"}},"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"1bee230a-7482-4012-b65d-638a94492de2"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260325001638619ba78a9a274716","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_f5a0735444934f8a916d3f05","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\n  --title \"proposal: Performance optimization and monitoring\" \\\n  --label \"proposal\" \\\n  --body \"## Proposal: Performance Optimization and Monitoring\n\n### Why\nAs the application grows and gains users, performance becomes increasingly important. Currently, there's no visibility into:\n- API response times\n- Database query performance\n- N+1 query issues\n- Frontend rendering performance\n- Resource usage patterns\n\n### Proposed Implementation\n\n#### Phase 1: Backend Performance\n1. **Add response time logging** to API routes\n2. **Database query logging** (slow query detection >100ms)\n3. **Add connection pooling** for SQLite (better-sqlite3-pool)\n4. **Optimize common queries** (add indexes where needed)\n\n#### Phase 2: Frontend Performance\n1. **Add Next.js build-time analysis** (\\`@next/bundle-analyzer\\`)\n2. **Optimize images** (use Next.js Image component properly)\n3. **Code splitting** for heavy components\n4. **Add loading skeletons** for better perceived performance\n\n#### Phase 3: Monitoring\n1. **Add performance metrics endpoint** (\\`/api/performance\\`)\n2. **Track key metrics**: \n   - Average response time by route\n   - Database query stats\n   - Memory usage\n   - Request rate\n3. **Add alerting** for performance degradation\n\n### Success Criteria\n- API response times < 200ms (p95)\n- Database queries < 50ms (p95)\n- Bundle size reduced by 20%+ (if possible)\n- Performance metrics visible in dashboard\n\n### Priority\n**P2** — Nice to have. The app performs well now, but this prepares it for scale and provides visibility into performance regressions.\n\n---\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\"","description":"Create performance optimization proposal"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"07254a5b-c53e-4611-b4bf-4843ee2ca23d"}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"command\":\"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\\\\n  --title \\\"proposal: Improve deployment reliability and monitoring\\\" \\\\\\n  --label \\\"proposal\\\" \\\\\\n  --body \\\"## Proposal: Deployment Reliability Improvements\\n\\n### Why\\nThe current deployment setup works but has some reliability gaps:\\n- No automated health checks after deployment\\n- PM2 doesn't auto-restart on certain failures\\n- No rollback mechanism if deployment fails\\n- No deployment history/visibility\\n\\n### Proposed Implementation\\n\\n#### 1. Post-Deploy Health Check\\nAdd automated health verification after deployment:\\n- Check \\\\`/api/health\\\\` endpoint\\n- Verify database connectivity\\n- Test critical user flows (login, browse listings)\\n- Rollback if health check fails\\n\\n#### 2. PM2 Improvements\\n- Enable \\\\`watch: false\\\\` (explicit, for production)\\n- Add \\\\`max_restarts: 10\\\\` limit\\n- Configure \\\\`min_uptime: '10s'\\\\` before considering app stable\\n- Add \\\\`autorestart: true\\\\` with exponential backoff\\n\\n#### 3. Deployment Enhancements\\n- Add \\\\`--rollback\\\\` flag to deploy script\\n- Keep last 3 builds available\\n- Add deployment logging with timestamps\\n- Send notification on deploy success/failure\\n\\n#### 4. Monitoring\\n- Add PM2 metrics monitoring (CPU, memory, restarts)\\n- Log rotation for PM2 logs\\n- Add uptime tracking\\n\\n### Success Criteria\\n- Failed deployments auto-rollback\\n- Health checks catch broken deploys\\n- Deployment history visible\\n- PM2 handles crashes gracefully\\n\\n### Priority\\n**P1** — Important for production stability. Prevents broken deploys from staying live and improves visibility.\\n\\n---\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\\\"\",\"description\":\"Create deployment reliability proposal\"}"}},"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"af5e32e3-8813-4d98-bb80-e6faeb335c55"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260325001647dfb260a528ce44ad","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_756462002e0c4e53a15b50f2","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\n  --title \"proposal: Improve deployment reliability and monitoring\" \\\n  --label \"proposal\" \\\n  --body \"## Proposal: Deployment Reliability Improvements\n\n### Why\nThe current deployment setup works but has some reliability gaps:\n- No automated health checks after deployment\n- PM2 doesn't auto-restart on certain failures\n- No rollback mechanism if deployment fails\n- No deployment history/visibility\n\n### Proposed Implementation\n\n#### 1. Post-Deploy Health Check\nAdd automated health verification after deployment:\n- Check \\`/api/health\\` endpoint\n- Verify database connectivity\n- Test critical user flows (login, browse listings)\n- Rollback if health check fails\n\n#### 2. PM2 Improvements\n- Enable \\`watch: false\\` (explicit, for production)\n- Add \\`max_restarts: 10\\` limit\n- Configure \\`min_uptime: '10s'\\` before considering app stable\n- Add \\`autorestart: true\\` with exponential backoff\n\n#### 3. Deployment Enhancements\n- Add \\`--rollback\\` flag to deploy script\n- Keep last 3 builds available\n- Add deployment logging with timestamps\n- Send notification on deploy success/failure\n\n#### 4. Monitoring\n- Add PM2 metrics monitoring (CPU, memory, restarts)\n- Log rotation for PM2 logs\n- Add uptime tracking\n\n### Success Criteria\n- Failed deployments auto-rollback\n- Health checks catch broken deploys\n- Deployment history visible\n- PM2 handles crashes gracefully\n\n### Priority\n**P1** — Important for production stability. Prevents broken deploys from staying live and improves visibility.\n\n---\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\"","description":"Create deployment reliability proposal"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"f099d8e6-d07a-4357-a2be-54d53776c403"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f69bd68a565449f4827043d7","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"body\":\"## Summary\\n- **Listing browse endpoint** no longer exposes `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id` — replaced `SELECT l.*` with explicit column list\\n- **Application reject** now uses `WHERE status='pending'` to prevent a concurrent approve from being silently overwritten by a reject\\n- **Register route** validates `email`, `password`, `displayName` are strings before calling `.length`/`.trim()` (was returning 500 on non-string input)\\n- **Apply route** wrapped in top-level try/catch for clean 500 responses on unexpected DB errors\\n- **Telegram/Discord link validation** uses strict regex requiring a path after the domain (rejects bare `https://t.me/`)\\n- **Reputation endpoint** rejects `userId=0` and negative values\\n- **fetchRatings** only fires once per page load via ref guard (prevents redundant API calls on every action)\\n\\n## Test plan\\n- [x] 207 tests pass (5 new: register type check, listings link exposure, reputation userId bounds)\\n- [x] ESLint clean\\n- [x] Build clean\\n- [x] Verified `GET /api/listings` response no longer contains invite link fields\\n\\nCloses #110\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\",\"reviews\":[],\"title\":\"fix: link exposure, reject race, type safety, validation (#110)\"}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"fe311004-ce9c-4a62-9cae-9d628fed1b37","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:20:35.002Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"{\"body\":\"## Summary\\n- **Listing browse endpoint** no longer exposes `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id` — replaced `SELECT l.*` with explicit column list\\n- **Application reject** now uses `WHERE status='pending'` to prevent a concurrent approve from being silently overwritten by a reject\\n- **Register route** validates `email`, `password`, `displayName` are strings before calling `.length`/`.trim()` (was returning 500 on non-string input)\\n- **Apply route** wrapped in top-level try/catch for clean 500 responses on unexpected DB errors\\n- **Telegram/Discord link validation** uses strict regex requiring a path after the domain (rejects bare `https://t.me/`)\\n- **Reputation endpoint** rejects `userId=0` and negative values\\n- **fetchRatings** only fires once per page load via ref guard (prevents redundant API calls on every action)\\n\\n## Test plan\\n- [x] 207 tests pass (5 new: register type check, listings link exposure, reputation userId bounds)\\n- [x] ESLint clean\\n- [x] Build clean\\n- [x] Verified `GET /api/listings` response no longer contains invite link fields\\n\\nCloses #110\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\",\"reviews\":[],\"title\":\"fix: link exposure, reject race, type safety, validation (#110)\"}","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260325002102f989122e0f764882","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I see - the commit still has session logs that contain secrets. I'll need to:\n1. Reset to the commit before the problematic one\n2. Make a new commit that excludes session logs entirely (they should be .gitignored)\n\nActually, looking at the gitignore earlier, I saw that `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/` was removed from .gitignore in commit 46b8719. The right solution is to re-add the sessions directory to .gitignore and then amend the commit.\n\nLet me check the current .gitignore:","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"bf8ce730-94cf-4910-9c58-f9608fc8b629"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9a2afa35a7e14ffa9b3330b2","type":"tool_result","content":"On branch main\nYour branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 1 commit.\n  (use \"git push\" to publish your local commits)\n\nChanges to be committed:\n  (use \"git restore --staged <file>...\" to unstage)\n\tnew file:   tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\n\tnew file:   ../package-lock.json\n\tnew file:   ../package.json\n\nChanges not staged for commit:\n  (use \"git add <file>...\" to update what will be committed)\n  (use \"git restore <file>...\" to discard changes in working directory)\n\tmodified:   ../.gitignore\n\nUntracked files:\n  (use \"git add <file>...\" to include in what will be committed)\n\t../..gitignore.swp\n\t../.claude/settings.local.json","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a70c7e1b-7788-4036-a7c2-454fad074e49","uuid":"d8111ba6-fbeb-4c5f-b048-531babce3f50","timestamp":"2026-03-24T16:27:00.714Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"On branch main\nYour branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 1 commit.\n  (use \"git push\" to publish your local commits)\n\nChanges to be committed:\n  (use \"git restore --staged <file>...\" to unstage)\n\tnew file:   tsconfig.tsbuildinfo\n\tnew file:   ../package-lock.json\n\tnew file:   ../package.json\n\nChanges not staged for commit:\n  (use \"git add <file>...\" to update what will be committed)\n  (use \"git restore <file>...\" to discard changes in working directory)\n\tmodified:   ../.gitignore\n\nUntracked files:\n  (use \"git add <file>...\" to include in what will be committed)\n\t../..gitignore.swp\n\t../.claude/settings.local.json","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
